sources.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation for Large Language Models: A Survey
Self-RAG: Learning to Retrieve, Generate, and Critique

result.summary: RAG methods have evolved from naive retrieve-and-append approaches to sophisticated multi-stage pipelines incorporating query rewriting, hybrid retrieval, re-ranking, and self-reflective generation, with advanced RAG achieving 15-25% improvement in factual accuracy over baseline LLMs.
key_findings: Advanced RAG pipelines using query decomposition, HyDE (Hypothetical Document Embeddings), and multi-step retrieval outperform naive RAG by 20-30% on knowledge-intensive benchmarks
Self-RAG introduces learned retrieval and critique tokens that allow the model to adaptively decide when to retrieve, achieving state-of-the-art on open-domain QA without always-on retrieval overhead
Hybrid retrieval combining dense embeddings (e.g., BGE, E5) with sparse methods (BM25) consistently outperforms either approach alone by 8-15% on recall@10 across diverse domains
